items.title: ICT Zone secures RM24.5mil hardware order
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ICT Zone Asia Bhd has secured a RM24.53 million purchase order for the supply of information and communication technology (ICT) hardware, including laptops and related peripheral devices. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group did not disclose the client but said its unit, ICT Zone Sdn Bhd, received the order from a Malaysia-based company involved in the distribution of ICT hardware and software. At the midday break, ICT Zone's share price was unchanged at 19 sen, with 542,600 shares traded, valuing the group at RM151.13 million.

items.title: Couple jailed one year for indecent act at Chinese cemetery in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 26 — A couple arrested for committing an indecent act at a Chinese cemetery in Penang was sentenced to one year’s jail each by the Magistrate’s Court in George Town, according to New Straits Times (NST). According to the report, Magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi handed down the sentence on M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, after they pleaded guilty to the offence under Section 377D of the Penal Code. Halila Abu Bakar, 37, is escorted by police after being sentenced to 12 months’ jail by the Magistrate’s Court.

items.title: King warns of health and fire risks, calls for precautions as Malaysia faces hot spell
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, has urged the Ministry of Education (MOE) to take serious measures to protect the health of students as the country grapples with a prolonged hot weather phenomenon. “His Majesty emphasised the importance of maintaining health by reducing outdoor activities that are exposed to the hot weather, as well as ensuring adequate water intake,” the message read. A special advisory was also issued for vulnerable groups, with the King advising that the elderly and children suffering from chronic illnesses not be exposed to the hot weather in order to avoid any complications.

items.title: How to end forgotten-child tragedies
items.summary: Most of these cases involve what researchers call "Forgotten Baby Syndrome" — a memory lapse driven by stress, fatigue, and breaks in routine. Although measures were proposed, these tragedies continue. Start with where people park.

items.title: Two men remanded four days in Johor Bahru over blaze that destroyed fireworks tent
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 26 — Two men, aged 30 and 35, have been remanded for four days starting today to assist in the investigation into a fire at a pop‑up tent selling fireworks along Jalan Bunga Ros in Kampung Dato’ Sulaiman Menteri here last Monday. Magistrate Chai Ing Hien granted the remand for the two suspects after receiving an application from the police at the Magistrate’s Court here this morning. Following the incident, police arrested two male suspects from a residential area in Kulai and the Kampung Melayu Majidee area here yesterday.

items.title: Southkey murder probe widens as sixth suspect remanded, four others’ remand extended in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 26 — A sixth suspect has been remanded for seven days starting today to assist in the murder probe of a foreigner who was killed in Southkey here on March 14. The same court also granted a one‑week remand extension until April 1 for four other suspects, including a woman, after their initial remand period ended today. Previously, the same court also granted an extension of the remand period for another suspect, which ended yesterday, until March 31.

items.title: RM2.6mil lost to motorcycle theft in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police recorded losses of about RM2.6 million from 322 motorcycle theft cases in Wangsa Maju last year, prompting a sustained crackdown that has crippled four active vehicle theft syndicates. Wangsa Maju police chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohamad Lazim Ismail said a nearly three-month intelligence-led operation under Op Lejang Khas 1/2026 led to the arrest of 21 individuals and the recovery of 24 stolen vehicles. The operation, conducted between Jan 1 and March 20 by the district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department, dismantled four syndicates identified as the Prakash, Ican, Kobis and Botak Kuang groups.

items.title: HLIB lowers Hiap Teck Venture's target price to 35 sen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB Research) has raised Hiap Teck Venture Bhd's core earnings forecasts after stronger-than-expected contributions from its joint venture in the second quarter (Q2). HLIB Research said the company's core earnings for the quarter ended Jan 31, 2026, rose 5.2 per cent to RM35.3 million, while year-on-year growth surged 33.5 per cent. The firm raised the company's FY26/27/28 core earnings forecasts by 12.1 per cent/16.6 per cent/12.5 per cent, respectively, reflecting higher earnings assumptions at Eastern Steel's level.

items.title: Venezuela's Maduro back in US court after stunning capture
items.summary: NEW YORK: Ousted Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro will appear Thursday in a New York court for the second time since his capture by US forces in an extraordinary nighttime raid. The stunning operation deposed the strongman who had led Venezuela since 2013 and has since forced the oil-rich country to largely bend to the will of US President Donald Trump. "The lawyers told us he is strong.

items.title: Will Sunway sweeten its IJM Corp offer as shareholders vote today?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: All eyes will be on Sunway Bhd this afternoon as it prepares to update the market on its proposed takeover of IJM Corp Bhd, a deal that has drawn mixed reactions and raised questions over valuation. The group is scheduled to convene its extraordinary general meeting at 3pm, where shareholders will vote on the RM3.15 per share offer for IJM, comprising a 10 per cent cash component and 90 per cent in new Sunway shares. However, the overall offer value remains unchanged.

items.title: Malaysia to ramp up domestic drug production support under NIMP 2030, BMI reports
items.summary: In a statement today, the Fitch Solutions research unit said Malaysia aims to increase the share of domestically-produced medical products under NIMP 2030, creating a more supportive policy backdrop for the local manufacturing of essential medicines, including generic medicines. “Together, these measures will accelerate the shift in public procurement toward locally produced, cost-effective generic medicines,” it said. Beyond lowering drug expenditure, the policy is designed to reshape clinicians’ prescribing habits and strengthen public confidence in generic medicines, said BMI.

items.title: Kelington earnings forecasts upgraded on strong orderbook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kelington Group Bhd is poised to record strong earnings growth notwithstanding the conflict in the Middle East, said RHB Research. "Financial year 2026 will see maiden earnings from the group's foray into the renewable energy sector. The firm also raised its forecasts for Kelington by 7.3 per cent, 11.4 per cent and 9.2 per cent for the financial years 2026, 2027, and 2028 (FY26/FY27/FY28.

items.title: Ferry operator reduces trips to Langkawi to stay afloat after diesel hike
items.summary: LANGKAWI: A ferry operator has reduced its daily trips to the island from five to three starting yesterday to ensure business sustainability and avoid laying off workers. The move follows a more than 100 per cent increase in industrial diesel prices, which has driven up operating costs and placed significant pressure on ferry operators in Langkawi. Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd general manager Dr Baharin Baharom said the company had no choice but to cut the number of trips.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh’s first 100 days: 13 reforms to strengthen governance and transparency in the Federal Territories
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has introduced 13 reform measures within her first 100 days in office to strengthen governance and increase administrative transparency in the capital. In a Facebook post today, she said the initiatives focus on improving administration and service delivery, including tackling tender cartels, widening public access to systems, and reinforcing checks and balances. She also conveyed appreciation to Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud, former Director-General Datuk Seri Noridah Abdul Rahim, Director Datuk Muhammad Azmi Mohd Zain, and the leadership of the Federal Territories Department for their support.

items.title: Retiree loses RM328,450 to 'police' phone scam in Pahang
items.summary: KUANTAN: A 64-year-old retiree has lost RM328,450 after falling victim to a phone scam involving a caller posing as a police officer. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim, from Rompin, received a WhatsApp call on Feb 4 from an individual claiming to be a police officer based in Putrajaya. The suspect alleged the victim was involved in a fraud case and would be arrested.

items.title: Diesel aid up to RM300 as prices hit RM5.52
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has increased cash assistance under the Budi Diesel programmes — Budi Individu and Budi Agri-Komoditi — by RM100, bringing the total to RM300. The move comes as diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia rose to RM5.52 per litre following a sharp increase in global oil prices. Prices in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan remain unchanged at RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Malaysia, Pakistan foreign ministers urge de‑escalation, stress diplomacy in West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysian Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and Pakistani Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar have underscored the need for de-escalation through dialogue and diplomacy amid the war in West Asia. In a post on X, Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said Dar and Mohamad discussed the war and issues of mutual interest during a telephone conversation. “The two leaders discussed recent regional and international developments of mutual interest and emphasised the importance of de-escalation, dialogue, and diplomacy to address ongoing challenges,” it said.

items.title: ETS, KTM Komuter services on Batu Caves–Pulau Sebang route fully restored
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad has announced that the Electric Train Service (ETS) and KTM Komuter operations on the Batu Caves-Pulau Sebang-Batu Caves route have resumed fully following earlier disruptions caused by damage to overhead line equipment (ATA). In a statement today, KTMB said repair works on the electrification system at KM 434.423 between Batang Benar and Labu stations were completed at 3.56 am today. KTMB confirmed that all affected ETS and KTM Komuter passengers have since safely reached their respective destinations.

items.title: Newborn baby found abandoned in dustbin in Ampang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A newborn baby girl was found abandoned in a dustbin in Taman Putra Ampang yesterday evening. Ampang Jaya police chief Khairul Anuar Khalid said police received a report at about 6.27pm from a local man who discovered the infant in a bin outside his house. Khairul Anuar said the infant was rushed to Ampang Hospital, where she is in stable condition and receiving treatment and monitoring.

items.title: Prices of vegetables likely to rise amid drought, conflict in the Middle East
items.summary: The Federation of Malaysian Vegetable Farmers Associations president, Ibrahim Roslan, said the dry spell has caused widespread crop losses. Ibrahim also said the Middle East conflict has contributed to rising fuel costs for diesel used in agriculture. "Soaring fertiliser and pesticide prices are also driving up production costs for farmers," he said.

items.title: Couple, son found dead in Sungai Petani house
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Three family members were found dead in their home at Kampung Belau, Merbok, here yesterday. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan is scheduled to hold a press conference regarding the case at the Merbok police station at noon today. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Government to intensify domestic drug production support under NIMP 2030: BMI
items.summary: In a statement today, the Fitch Solutions research unit said Malaysia aims to increase the share of domestically-produced medical products under NIMP 2030, creating a more supportive policy backdrop for the local manufacturing of essential medicines, including generic medicines. "Together, these measures will accelerate the shift in public procurement toward locally produced, cost-effective generic medicines," it said. Beyond lowering drug expenditure, the policy is designed to reshape clinicians' prescribing habits and strengthen public confidence in generic medicines, said BMI.

items.title: PKR's Abu Hafiz challenges Victor Chin to name MP in RM9.5mil bribe allegation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR Central Leadership Council member Dr Abu Hafiz Salleh Hudin has challenged businessman Victor Chin to "stop the drama" and publicly identify the member of parliament who allegedly extorted RM9.5 million from him. On Tuesday, Chin alleged that a PKR MP asked for RM10 million in cash to settle the matter. Yesterday, PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan denied allegations linking him to Chin and the alleged corporate mafia.

items.title: Dark-coloured waste, vandalism force closure of Johor Baru water park
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The water park at Hutan Bandar has been temporarily closed for repairs following acts of vandalism, the Johor Baru City Council (MBJB) said. In a post on its official Facebook page, the council said the closure was necessary to ensure public safety and to allow maintenance work on affected facilities. MBJB said the water park is a shared public facility that must be used responsibly.

items.title: Three cops hurt in scuffle at George Town flats [WATCH]
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Three policemen ended up with minor injuries after they were involved in a scuffle with several individuals during an enforcement operation at a flat in Jalan Sungai here last night. Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said the incident happened after the policemen rushed to the area following a request for assistance from personnel on the ground who encountered a hostile crowd while pursuing a suspect. Several video footages of the incident have since gone viral since last night.

items.title: HSBC sees Malaysia's economy staying on solid growth path in 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: HSBC Private Bank expects Malaysia's economy to maintain solid growth in 2026, despite short-term risks from global uncertainties. HSBC Private Bank and Premier Wealth investment strategist Abhilash Narayan said strong domestic consumption, continued infrastructure and data centre investment, and a recovery in tourism continue to be supportive for the country's economic growth this year. In its second-quarter (Q2) 2026 investment outlook, HSBC Private Bank forecasts Malaysia's real gross domestic product to grow 4.5 per cent this year, slightly moderating from 4.9 per cent in 2025.

items.title: Golden Destinations targets RM90mil in IPO on ACE Market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Golden Destinations Group Bhd aims to raise RM90 million through its initial public offering (IPO) as part of its listing on the Ace Market. The IPO is priced at 45 sen a share, giving the company an estimated market capitalisation of RM450 million upon listing. Its IPO comprises the issuance of 200 million new shares and 100 million offer for sale of existing shares.

items.title: Couple jailed one year for indecent act at Penang cemetery [WATCH]
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A couple, arrested for an indecent act at a Chinese cemetery, was sentenced to one year's jail by the magistrate's court today after they pleaded guilty to the offence. Magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi handed down the sentence on M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, after they admitted to the charge under Section 377D of the Penal Code. Lawyer representing both accused, R. Purantharan, told the court that the first accused, Halila, is single and unemployed.

items.title: MTT Shipping and Logistics prices IPO at RM1.03 per share, raising RM652.5mil
items.summary: The IPO comprises 633.5 million issue shares, with 312.5 million shares allocated for Bumiputera investors approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI), 258.5 million shares for Malaysian and foreign institutional investors, and 12.5 million shares reserved for the company directors, employees, and key contributors. The remaining 50 million shares will be reserved for the Malaysian public via balloting, split evenly between Bumiputera and non-Bumiputera. The remaining 4.3 per cent, or RM27.8 million, will be allocated for the reimbursement of listing expenses within the first 6 months upon listing.

items.title: Open fires dominate emergency lines, accounting for up to 60pct of calls
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Up to 60 per cent of emergency calls received by the fire and rescue department during the hot season are linked to open fires, including bush, forest and plantation blazes, authorities say. Fire and Rescue Department Director-general Datuk Seri Ts. "Bush and grass fires remain the largest contributor, with 26,800 cases, followed by forest fires (3,860) and farm and plantation fires (2,150)," he told Kosmo!.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM MAR 26, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's 30-stock index opened higher today but later pared gains, despite Wall Street closing on a positive note overnight. Analysts anticipate some bargain hunting could support the market as global sentiment turns slightly bullish amid easing US-Iran tensions. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin climbed to RM283,498.

items.title: Fuel supply at petrol stations nationwide remains sufficient, says petrol dealers group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fuel supply at petrol stations nationwide remains sufficient, the Petrol Dealers Association of Malaysia (PDAM) said. PDAM confirmed that operational conditions at Malaysian petrol stations are stable, with supply deliveries proceeding smoothly and without technical disruptions. "The ministry continuously monitors petrol stations.

items.title: French court finds Swiss Islamic scholar Ramadan guilty of rape
items.summary: PARIS: Swiss Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan was sentenced on Wednesday to 18 years in prison by a Paris criminal court for the rape of three women. A charismatic yet controversial figure in European Islam, Ramadan has always maintained his innocence. In 2025, Switzerland's supreme court rejected Ramadan's appeal against his conviction for the rape of a woman in a Geneva hotel nearly two decades ago.

items.title: PLUS urges Raya travellers to plan return trips as 2.3 million vehicles expected on highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad has urged motorists to plan their return journeys to the Klang Valley using its MyPLUS-TTA digital travel schedule amid an expected surge in post-Hari Raya traffic. “Planning return journeys according to these recommended travel times will significantly assist in alleviating traffic congestion along the highway,” it said. PLUS said it will activate Smart Lanes at 34 strategic locations and deploy PLUSRonda teams for 24-hour patrols to assist motorists.

items.title: Takiyuddin tells MACC, police to probe RM10mil bribe claims involving PKR MP [WATCH]
items.summary: Takiyuddin also called on the government, particularly PKR leaders, to explain the allegations and fully cooperate with investigators. On Tuesday, businessman Victor Chin, also known as Chin Boon Loong and linked to a "corporate mafia" scandal, alleged that a PKR MP asked for RM10 million in cash to settle the matter. LINK: https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/1398850/corporate-mafia-probe-why-raid-my-house-asks-victor-chin Yesterday, PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan denied allegations linking him to Chin and the alleged corporate mafia.

items.title: Couple arrive in Penang court to face indecency charges [WATCH]
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A couple arrested for an indecent act at a local Chinese cemetery arrived at the magistrate's court to face charges. Police launched an investigation after a 69-year-old man reported a video showing a man and woman behaving inappropriately at the cemetery. The 38-second video had gone viral, showing a man standing naked with his back to the camera, while a partially undressed woman sits on a tombstone.

items.title: US jury finds Meta, YouTube liable in social media addiction trial
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: A Los Angeles jury on Wednesday found Meta and YouTube liable for harming a young woman because of an addictive design of their social media platforms, ordering the companies to pay $6 million in damages, including $3 million in punitive damages. The panel awarded $3 million in compensatory damages, assigning Meta 70 percent of the responsibility for the plaintiff's harm – a $2.1 million share – and YouTube the remaining 30 percent, or $900,000. Both companies said they would appeal the verdict.

items.title: Stocks on edge as Middle East ceasefire talks take centre stage
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Asian stocks struggled for direction while the dollar held firm on Thursday as investors treaded cautiously amid dizzying developments in the Middle East, where Iran said it would weigh a US proposal to end the Gulf conflict. The widening war has jolted global markets, sending oil prices soaring, reigniting inflation fears and scrambling global rate expectations. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan edged 0.23 per cent lower, set for an 8.7 per cent decline in the month, its biggest monthly drop since October 2022.

items.title: Anwar expresses support for Qatar, condemns attack on Ras Laffan LNG facility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysia has firmly expressed its support for Qatar’s sovereignty and strongly condemned the attack targeting the Ras Laffan Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) facility, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said that in a telephone conversation with Qatar’s Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, Malaysia described the energy infrastructure as critical to the global economy. a Anwar said that he and Sheikh Mohammed also exchanged Aidilfitri greetings and expressed concern over the worsening security situation in West Asia.

items.title: Retiree collects thousands of discarded lighters to decorate home
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A 71-year-old, Ismail Hassan, has turned his Kampung Kuala Bekah home into a vibrant, plastic-paved landmark through his unique approach to recycling. While others sort their waste into bins, the retiree has spent the last three years adorning his walls with nearly 4,000 discarded lighters. Ismail cycles daily to local wakaf (pavilions) and public resting spots, where spent lighters are often discarded.

items.title: Police detain man over viral Facebook post insulting Prophet Muhammad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Police detained a 47-year-old local man suspected of insulting Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) over a Facebook post that recently went viral. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said a report was received at 1.30 am last Sunday regarding the offensive post uploaded by an account using the name ‘Sakti Sakti Sakti’. He said the suspect was arrested by a police team at 8.32 pm yesterday in Butterworth, Penang.

items.title: Selangor police chief: Viral Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026 fundraiser is scam, not our event
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 26 — Selangor police have denied the involvement of its Escort Unit in organising a Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026 event that went viral in a message circulating yesterday. Its police chief, Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the message, which claimed the event would be held tomorrow at the Selangor police contingent headquarters hall and solicited donations to a MBSB Bank account under the name Norfadilah Md Nor, is false. “This is a scam attempt to reap profit and tarnish the image of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM),” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Police find skeletal remains of baby buried in Alor Gajah house compound, four arrested
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 26 —Police found the skeletal remains of a baby, believed to be four months old, buried in the compound of a house here yesterday. He said police have also arrested four individuals, including two women, to assist in the investigation. The presence of a police forensics team caused a stir in the residential area of Kelemak here at around 9 am.

items.title: Nine-year-old autistic boy drowns in hotel lake in Lukut, Port Dickson
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 26 — A nine-year-old autistic boy was found drowned in a lake within a hotel area in Lukut near here after he was believed to have left his hotel room without his family’s knowledge yesterday. Port Dickson police chief Supt Maslan Udin said a report on the incident was received at 8.06 pm from a 44-year-old family member of the victim. He said the child was reported missing at about 3.30 pm after he was believed to have left the hotel room unnoticed.

items.title: Anwar calls for Lebanon to be included in expanded West Asia ceasefire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysia has called for a ceasefire in the ongoing West Asian conflict to be expanded to include Lebanon, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the call was conveyed during a telephone conversation with Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, noting that the country has been severely affected, with about one million people displaced and more than 1,000 fatalities recorded. “I reaffirmed Malaysia’s support for Lebanon’s sovereignty, condemned attacks on civilian infrastructure and urged all parties to de-escalate tensions and return to dialogue, while also addressing the increasingly urgent humanitarian situation,” he said in a Facebook post yesterday During the conversation, the Prime Minister also extended Aidilfitri greetings to his counterpart and expressed appreciation for the Lebanese government’s support in ensuring the safety and welfare of personnel from the Malaysian Battalion (MALBATT) serving under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL).

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia, Singapore concerned over Strait of Hormuz closure amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysia and Singapore have expressed serious concern over the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on critical infrastructure following the escalating conflict in West Asia, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on critical infrastructure could disrupt global oil market stability and regional energy security, particularly in Southeast Asia. “Malaysia stands ready to support any efforts to de-escalate tensions and restore peace, justice and stability in West Asia,” he said.

items.title: Concrete capillaries: How hidden viaduct passages enable MRT cable thieves to access tracks in the Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Beneath the elevated tracks, a hidden network of hollow spaces within Klang Valley’s MRT viaducts is being exploited by cable thieves, inadvertently turning critical infrastructure into a pathway for intrusion. “In the past, cable thieves mainly targeted the ground-level tracks, but now they have also started attacking our elevated cables by entering through the viaducts and concrete piers. Hillside terrain and ground-level access near the Phileo Damansara station on the MRT Kajang Line provide natural cover and multiple informal entry points.

items.title: JKR Sarawak says viral ‘vehicle damage claim’ checklist is fake
items.summary: SIBU, March 25 — The Sarawak Public Works Department (JKR) has denied issuing or circulating a document titled “Checklist of Documents for Vehicle Damage Compensation Claims” that has been shared on social media. In a statement released today by its corporate communications branch, JKR Sarawak stressed that the document is invalid and was not issued by the department. According to the statement, affected road users can contact the department’s official channels, including the Sarawak Public Works Department Facebook page, the TaliKhidmat portal at https://talikhidmat.sarawak.gov.my, the JKR Sarawak Care WhatsApp line at +60 11-7017 1777, or email corporatejkr@gmail.com for further complaints and enquiries.

items.title: Anwar welcomes Pakistan’s proposal to host US–Iran dialogue, says diplomacy must prevail (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed Pakistan’s timely and constructive offer to host a dialogue between the United States (US) and Iran amid a period of acute regional danger. Anwar said Malaysia supports the initiative and encouraged the US and Iran to respond in the spirit in which it was offered. The region deserves something more durable,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia’s universities improve in QS global subject score, but UM’s slide tells different story
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Malaysia’s universities recorded an overall 18 per cent net improvement in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026, but beneath that headline figure lies a story that is far more complicated. While specialist institutions like Universiti Teknologi Petronas and UCSI University surged forward, the country’s most comprehensive research university, Universiti Malaya, posted a net improvement rate of negative 25 per cent — the starkest divergence in the national results. UM entered more subjects than any other Malaysian university, fielding 40 narrow subject entries and five broad faculty areas, giving it the widest footprint in the rankings by some distance.

items.title: Fire Dept: Punggai Bypass bush fire 80pc contained as crews battle heat and strong winds
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 25 — Firefighting operations involving a bush fire covering an estimated 150 hectares along the Punggai Bypass Highway towards Sebana Cove have reached 80 per cent containment as of 1pm today. Punggai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Senior Fire Officer II Ezam Mistorerrodin said about 30 hectares remain active and are still being extinguished. He said the fire spread rapidly due to hot and dry weather, as well as strong winds, which have hampered firefighting efforts.

items.title: Malaysia’s UTP cracks global top 10 in historic QS university rankings breakthrough
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Universiti Teknologi Petronas has achieved what no Malaysian university has ever done before, ranking ninth in the world for Petroleum Engineering in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026. The landmark result, released by global higher education analysts QS Quacquarelli Symonds today, marks the first time any Malaysian institution has broken into the top 10 of any subject in the rankings’ sixteen-year history. QS senior vice president Ben Sowter credited the result to deliberate, long-term policy decisions “Malaysia’s rising performance in the QS subject rankings, including its highest-ever rank, achieved by UTP in Petroleum Engineering, reflects the long-term impact of reforms set out in the Malaysia Education Blueprint 2015-2025 and its continuation in the new Malaysia Higher Education Blueprint 2026-2035,” Sowter said in the announcement.

items.title: Sarawak police fast‑tracking investigation into Sri Aman crash that killed three
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — Police will expedite investigations into a fatal accident in Sri Aman that claimed the lives of three family members recently. Sarawak deputy police commissioner DCP Saifullizan Ishak said the investigation is being conducted from multiple angles. The accident on March 20 involved a Proton X70 and a Perodua Alza along Jalan Serian-Sri Aman.

items.title: Buying fuel in containers? You need a special permit, says Domestic Trade Ministry following viral drum‑filling cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Companies, organisations and individuals intending to purchase petrol or diesel at fuel stations for use outside vehicle tanks are required to obtain a Special Permit from the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) under the Supply Control Act 1961. “Permit holders may store the fuel in drums, plastic containers or other suitable and safe containers, subject to the approved quantity, fuel station location, duration and frequency of purchase,” he said in a statement today. “No parties other than Special Permit holders are allowed to purchase petrol or diesel in containers at fuel stations, except in emergency cases, where the permitted quantity is below 20 litres,” he said.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Police followed SOPs in arresting teens over terror‑related activities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) action in arresting individuals, including underage teenagers believed to be attempting to revive a terrorist group ideology in the country, was carried out in accordance with standard operating procedures (SOP), said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Saifuddin Nasution said investigations found that the teenagers had been accessing websites related to violence. Meanwhile, in addressing potential long-term implications of the West Asian conflict, Saifuddin Nasution said the Cabinet has tasked the Home Ministry and the Defence Ministry with developing scenario planning to ensure national security remains intact.

items.title: Meru police station in Klang introduces bell‑entry system for after‑hours access following brawl
items.summary: KLANG, March 25 — As a security measure, the Meru police station is now using a bell system to control public entry after midnight, following a brawl at the station on March 2. North Klang district police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said this measure was taken to strengthen security aspects at the station, apart from ensuring the safety of police personnel and the public. “This measure is important because there are still many police stations across the country operating in shoplots like the Meru police station and requiring more effective security control.

items.title: Malaysia keeps subsidised RON95 at RM1.99, raises RON97 and diesel prices after global oil surge
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Malaysia will maintain the subsidised price of RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre under the Budi95 scheme, while prices of RON97 and diesel have been adjusted following a surge in global oil prices. Under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism, retail fuel prices for the week of March 26 to April 1 are set at RM5.15 per litre for RON97, RM3.87 for unsubsidised RON95, RM5.52 for diesel in Peninsular Malaysia, and RM2.15 for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan. The ministry said the government remains committed to protecting consumers despite volatile global markets, noting that Malaysia’s domestic fuel prices are influenced by international supply as much of it is imported.

items.title: No Malaysian peacekeepers harmed in south Lebanon explosions, says defence minister
items.summary: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the incidents occurred following tensions between the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) and Hezbollah in the area. “The Defence Ministry, through the Joint Forces Headquarters, is constantly monitoring the latest developments, particularly in MALBATT 850-13 operational areas. The safety of personnel remains a priority,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Mersing teen found drowned in Mersing river, body recovered 100m from last known location
items.summary: Mersing police chief Supt Abdul Razak Abdullah Sani said the body of Muhammad Adam Haziq Muhammad Hazriq was discovered at 12.41 pm. He said the Fire and Rescue Department’s Water Rescue Unit (PPDA) found the body about 100 metres from where the boy was reported to have gone missing, and at a depth of about six metres. Meanwhile, Endau Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) Operations Commander, Senior Fire Officer II Mohd Sahrel Anuar Mawarday, said they received an emergency call at 9.24 pm (on March 24) and dispatched six personnel to the scene.

items.title: Johor police nab sixth suspect in Southkey murder with help from Singapore
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 25 — Police have arrested another suspect to assist in the investigation into the murder of a man in Southkey here on March 14, bringing the total number of arrests so far to six individuals. Johor deputy police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said the sixth suspect, a 30-year-old foreign man, was detained in Singapore this morning through close cooperation between the Royal Malaysia Police and the republic’s authorities. “A remand application for the suspect will be made tomorrow,” he told a press conference at the assembly held to commemorate the 219th Police Day at the Johor police contingent headquarters (IPK) here today.
